Name: adaptive_kernel_mixed_precision_quantization
Title: Enhancing Inference Efficiency through Adaptive Kernel-Level Mixed-Precision Quantization
Short Hypothesis: Can we achieve significant improvements in inference efficiency by dynamically optimizing mixed-precision configurations at the kernel level based on real-time profiling metrics from GPU execution?
Related Work: While existing works like MxMoE and HMQAT focus on theoretical frameworks or heuristic methods for mixed-precision quantization, they often fail to account for the actual hardware performance characteristics. AKMPQ distinguishes itself by emphasizing empirical kernel-level optimization and real-time profiling, allowing for adaptive adjustments that directly impact wall-clock latency and throughput on specific hardware.
Abstract: The rapid evolution of deep learning models has led to significant computational and memory demands, necessitating innovative strategies for optimizing inference efficiency. This proposal introduces Adaptive Kernel-Level Mixed-Precision Quantization (AKMPQ), an empirical framework that dynamically adjusts mixed-precision configurations based on real-time profiling metrics gathered from GPU executions. By focusing on kernel-level throughput engineering, AKMPQ aims to optimize data types, kernel configurations, and memory layouts, ensuring that the deployed models not only meet accuracy requirements but also achieve unprecedented levels of performance. We will conduct extensive experiments to validate our approach on commodity GPUs, utilizing metrics such as p50/p99 latency, throughput under batching, and energy per token. Our findings will contribute to the growing body of knowledge in efficient model deployment and provide practitioners with actionable insights into optimizing mixed-precision quantization strategies.
Experiments: Baseline Establishment: Measure the performance of standard mixed-precision quantization techniques on benchmark models (e.g., ResNet, LLMs) using profiling tools (nvprof/Nsight).
Dynamic Profiling: Implement a real-time profiling system that monitors kernel execution to gather metrics (occupancy, bandwidth utilization, stall reasons).

Adaptive Configuration: Develop an algorithm that adjusts mixed-precision configurations based on the profiling data collected, testing various strategies (e.g., layer-wise precision adjustment, data type optimization).
Performance Evaluation: Compare the latency, throughput, and energy consumption of AKMPQ against baseline methods and report the results via comprehensive profiling traces.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Complexity of Implementation: Developing a dynamic profiling and adjustment system may introduce complexities that could affect reproducibility.
Hardware Variability: Results may vary across different GPU architectures, limiting generalizability.
Measurement Overhead: Real-time profiling might introduce additional overhead that could affect performance metrics.
